talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Washington University School of Medicine in St. Louis
talk_metadata.talk_title: Risk-based breast cancer screening: evidence, gaps, and a proposed framework
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we have no consistent map of risk and screening frequency"
Speaker said: "risk-based screening improves benefits and reduces harms. Is the case closed?"
Speaker said: "we're not saying my model's better than yours and Gail's not as good as Tyrer-Cuzick"
Speaker said: "we're not there. Obviously, the trials are not consistent in even defining the categories"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of a unified risk threshold and inconsistent interval definitions across trials

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Breast cancer risk stratification based on five-year absolute risk
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No consistent cut‑point for defining high‑risk; uses composite or varying definitions across studies
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Standardized molecular or imaging biomarker to harmonize risk categorization
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Underpowered to detect differences due to heterogeneous risk definitions

trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Uses a lifetime risk cut‑point that does not translate to a clear five‑year risk threshold for screening decisions
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Validated absolute risk calculator calibrated to local incidence

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Risk model algorithms (e.g., Gail, Tyrer‑Cuzick, image‑based models)
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted lack of consensus and calibration across models, suggesting no single proprietary algorithm dominates the field
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing broad, model‑agnostic recommendations without a validated, universally accepted risk threshold may dilute clinical impact

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that risk‑based models can reduce false positives by up to a quarter, then concluded that the approach is not ready for primetime clinical implementation

key_data_gaps: No randomized trial demonstrating clinical outcomes of risk‑based intervals
No standardized risk cut‑points across studies
Lack of prospective validation in diverse populations

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a unified, calibrated absolute risk biomarker panel (e.g., integrating imaging‑derived risk with genomic and clinical data) to define consistent cut‑points for screening intervals
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If we could get a good measure of absolute risk, how do we frame the screening, starting and frequency to actually get to that population benefit?"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omic and imaging features to generate a composite risk score with validated thresholds

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a prospective, risk‑stratified screening trial using a single, pre‑specified risk threshold and uniform interval definitions
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "We have no consistent map of risk and screening frequency"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial outcomes under various risk thresholds to optimize design before launch

cited_competitors.name: Luna
cited_competitors.context: Consulting for software licensed from WashU

cited_competitors.name: NCCN
cited_competitors.context: Recently updated guidelines to include image‑based risk

unresolved_questions: What specific absolute risk cut‑point should trigger a shift from biennial to annual screening?
How can risk models be calibrated across diverse populations to avoid widening disparities?

assets_to_track: Risk‑based breast cancer screening trials (e.g., MAPEBES, Perspective)

talk_metadata.talk_title: Precision screening and surveillance for colorectal cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Only 64% of patients with high‑risk polyps actually received the surveillance"
Speaker said: "Compliance in terms of the interval of the surveillance is only 21%"
Speaker said: "Polygenic risk score is strongly associated with higher risk of CRC among individuals who have received a colonoscopy"
Speaker said: "Current risk assessment tools have suboptimal accuracy (AUC ~0.63) leading to modest cost‑effectiveness"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Current colorectal cancer risk assessment tools
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low predictive accuracy (AUC ~0.63) and lack of integration of genetics, lifestyle, and colonoscopy quality metrics
Patient and provider workflow gaps leading to only 21% compliance with recommended surveillance intervals
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker said: "Only 64% of patients with high‑risk polyps actually received the surveillance"

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Effectiveness of surveillance intervals after high‑risk polyp detection
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Real‑world data without standardized surveillance protocol leads to heterogeneous follow‑up
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Incorporation of polygenic risk scores and ADR quality metrics into surveillance decision rules
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential confounding by indication and variable colonoscopy quality

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Existing guideline‑driven surveillance schedules based solely on polyp pathology
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker highlighted that recommendations are based on low‑quality evidence and lead to both over‑ and under‑use
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on histopathology alone without genetics or quality metrics limits personalization

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker presented data that high‑quality colonoscopy (high ADR) dramatically lowers post‑colonoscopy CRC risk, yet concluded that risk‑based screening remains limited by modest AUC of current models

key_data_gaps: Prospective validation of combined PRS + colonoscopy quality model
Cost‑effectiveness analyses incorporating AI‑enhanced risk scores

crispro_opportunity.description: Integrate polygenic risk scores with colonoscopy quality (ADR) and polyp pathology to refine surveillance intervals
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Polygenic risk score is strongly associated with higher risk of CRC among individuals who have received a colonoscopy" and "Patients with high‑risk polyps and low ADR have over a threefold increased risk"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model multi‑dimensional risk (genomics, procedural quality, histology) to generate individualized surveillance recommendations

crispro_opportunity.description: Apply AI‑driven image analysis to colonoscopy videos to improve ADR and reduce interval cancers
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted: "AI has been widely used in colonoscopy... computer‑aided colonoscopy can help physicians better spot polyps"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s computational spine can incorporate AI‑derived detection metrics as quantitative inputs for risk modeling

crispro_opportunity.description: Design a prospective, risk‑stratified colonoscopy surveillance trial using combined PRS and ADR thresholds
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted low adherence and modest predictive performance of current tools
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Simulate trial power and endpoint outcomes under various risk‑stratification schemas

cited_competitors.name: NCCN
cited_competitors.context: Updated guidelines to include image‑based risk for breast cancer (mentioned for contrast)

cited_competitors.name: UK Biobank
cited_competitors.context: Source of validation data for polygenic risk score

unresolved_questions: How can a unified risk score be operationalized in routine clinical workflows without adding burden?
What is the cost‑effectiveness threshold for adding PRS to standard surveillance protocols?

assets_to_track: Polygenic risk score panels for colorectal cancer
companies_to_monitor: Companies developing ADR‑tracking and AI colonoscopy tools
